npcs.name: Marcus
npcs.title: a weary drifter
npcs.arrivalLine: A tired man in a torn coat taps three times on the hatch.
npcs.atDoorLine: "Please... I've been walking for days. I just need a few hours of rest."

intro.text: Marcus settles onto the floor, rubbing his hands. "Thank you. It's been weeks out there. Is this place safe?"

choices.text: Safe enough. What's your name, stranger?
We manage. Where did you come from?
Don't get comfortable. I may ask you to leave.

name.text: "Marcus. I used to repair radios before the cities fell. If you've got a receiver, I might be able to pull signals."

choices.text: We have one. You could take a look later.
We don't trust broadcasts anymore.
I think it's time you moved on.

origin.text: "The old highway. Walked past burning cars and empty towns. Saw things I don't want to describe."

choices.text: Did you see any survivors?
How close are the swarms now?
Enough talk. You need to go.

warn.text: Marcus nods slowly. "Understood. I'll take whatever time you can give."

choices.text: Tell me who you are, then.
Where did you come from?
Actually — go. Now.

radio.text: "A working radio in these times is a miracle. I owe you one for letting me listen."

choices.text: Just don't touch anything without asking.
You can listen. Nothing more.
On second thought, it's time for you to go.

quiet.text: "Fair enough. Silence is safer anyway." He rests his head against the wall.

choices.text: Get some sleep. We'll talk later.
Actually — I'd prefer you left.
Tell me how you survived out there.

survivors.text: "A few. Most are hiding in basements or attics. Some banded together — not all of them are friendly."

choices.text: Did any of them follow you here?
We should trade with the friendly ones.
That's enough. You need to leave.

swarms.text: "Close. A few miles west. The night draws them out — stay quiet and you'll be fine."

choices.text: Thanks for the warning.
How do you know so much?
You've outstayed your welcome.

trust.text: Marcus gives a small, tired smile. "I appreciate the trust. I won't abuse it."

choices.text: Rest now. We'll sort things out tomorrow.
Tell me more about what's out there.
Actually — I need you to go.

sleep.text: Marcus closes his eyes. For a moment the bunker feels a little less alone.

choices.text: Watch him quietly.
Wake him. He needs to leave.
Let him rest. Return to your duties.

askleave.text: You ask Marcus to leave. He looks at you for a long moment. "After everything? Please — let me explain myself first."

choices.text: I'm sorry. Our supplies are thin and I have to protect this place. Please go peacefully.
Get out. I'm done being polite.
Maybe just one more night?

npcs.name: Nora
npcs.title: a sharp-eyed scavenger
npcs.arrivalLine: A short woman with a dusty backpack raps on the hatch.
npcs.atDoorLine: "I can trade — batteries, filters, anything. Just let me in before the light goes."

intro.text: Nora sets her pack down carefully. "Thanks. Nice setup you have here. Secure too."

choices.text: What do you have to trade?
Where did you find all this?
I'm keeping my distance. Don't get comfortable.

trade.text: "Mostly salvage. Lithium cells, water filters, some canned goods. You help me, I help you."

choices.text: Show me the cells.
We're self-sufficient. Thanks.
Actually — I'd rather you moved on.

origin.text: "Old pharmacy, two towns over. Didn't meet anything living on the way in. Didn't meet anything dead, either."

choices.text: Any rumors of what's out there?
Show me what you have to trade.
You should go.

warn.text: "Fair enough. I've been kicked out of worse places. I'll behave."

choices.text: Let's start with what you have.
Tell me where you've been.
On second thought — go now.

deal.text: She unrolls a cloth — two charged cells, a pack of filters, a roll of copper wire. Honest pricing.

choices.text: Good. Let's work out an exchange.
I'll think about it.
Not interested. Please go.

quiet.text: "Alright. I'll keep out of your way. Wake me if anything goes wrong."

choices.text: Rest up.
What have you been hearing on the roads?
Actually — I need you to leave.

rumors.text: "Some say the swarms aren't just zombies anymore. Lights in the sky. Broken machines moving on their own. I try not to listen."

choices.text: That's a lot to take in.
We'll watch the skies tonight.
Enough stories. Please leave.

rest.text: Nora nods off against her pack. Even asleep she keeps one hand on the strap.

choices.text: Let her sleep.
Wake her. She has to leave.
Check her pack quietly.

askleave.text: You tell Nora it's time to move on. She raises an eyebrow. "You sure about that? I'm useful to have around."

choices.text: I appreciate what you brought, but this bunker can't support two. Travel safe.
Out. Before I regret letting you in.
Just... stay out of my way, then.

npcs.name: Elias
npcs.title: a soft-spoken medic
npcs.arrivalLine: An older man in a faded red cross jacket calls out from above the hatch.
npcs.atDoorLine: "I'm a medic. I can patch wounds, stitches, antibiotics — I just need somewhere to rest."

intro.text: Elias breathes out slowly as the hatch seals. "It's quiet in here. Thank you for the kindness."

choices.text: You said you're a medic?
Where did you walk from?
You can stay briefly — that's it.

medic.text: "Thirty-seven years of field work. Most of it before the outbreak. I don't charge, if that's what you're wondering."

choices.text: We're lucky you found us.
Have you treated anyone infected?
You'll still need to move on soon.

origin.text: "There was a clinic north of here. Overrun two nights ago. I got the children out, but not much else."

choices.text: Where are they now?
Any of them infected?
I'm sorry. But you need to keep going.

warn.text: "I understand completely. Safety first. I'll only take what I need."

choices.text: Tell me about yourself.
Where are you headed?
Actually — leave now.

kind.text: "You remind me why I kept the jacket on. Kindness is still out here. It's quieter, but it's here."

choices.text: Rest, then. We'll talk tomorrow.
Tell me what else you've seen.
I have to ask you to leave.

infected.text: "The early stages look like flu. By the time the bite shows, it's too late. If anyone here shows symptoms, I can help."

choices.text: Hopefully we never need that.
We'll keep an eye on it.
Please — it's time for you to go.

rest.text: Elias folds his jacket into a pillow and lays his head down. He falls asleep instantly.

choices.text: Let him rest.
Wake him. He needs to leave.
Sit with him for a while.

askleave.text: You tell Elias it's time. He stands without protest, but his eyes are sad. "Is there anything I can say to change your mind?"

choices.text: I truly wish you safety. Take this water for the road — please go.
No. Leave. Don't come back.
I don't know. Maybe one more day?
